refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying what is required without how to achieve it. There are no multiple obligations to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Establish real-time communication quality monitoring with feedback control to dynamically manage network resources
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network condition monitoring accuracy
Interaction between adaptive protocol and error correction mechanisms if combined
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain packet loss limits by adapting transmission parameters, which can be modularized independently from other system functions. It avoids coupling with specific hardware or network technologies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate network condition data to inform adaptation
Risk that adaptation may not fully compensate for sudden network degradation
Assumes that operational policies allow dynamic adjustment of communication parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including error detection and correction as a complementary DP or as part of the adaptive protocol to enhance robustness against packet loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to monitor network performance metrics and adjust transmission parameters accordingly

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network layer for sending and receiving data packets

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling; minimal diffusion since protocol adjustments are localized here.
Low containment as vehicle module focuses on physical and control systems; would require extensive diffusion to network-related functions.
Partial containment possible since teleoperation depends on communication, but adaptive protocol logic is not central here.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, unrelated to communication protocol adaptation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate protocol logic with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause minor coupling.
If network link interface changes, adaptation logic may require updates across modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of packet loss ≤ 1%.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause minor coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment for the adaptive communication protocol with minimal diffusion risk. Other modules have low containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring compliance with the customer-defined packet loss limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly encapsulates the adaptive communication protocol implementation, with well-defined interfaces to Telemetry and Network Link, providing clear allocation of responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the dependency on telemetry data to minimize coupling and ensure interface stability.
Establish interface versioning or abstraction layers for the Network Link to reduce impact of changes on the adaptive protocol logic.
Include explicit verification steps in the worksite safety plan and operational policies to ensure packet loss criteria are met during teleoperation.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The adaptive communication protocol meets the packet loss criteria with clear ownership and feasible verification. Although minor risks related to telemetry data dependency and interface stability exist, these are manageable through recommended revisions and do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and containment at this level is preferable to avoid complexity and potential diffusion of responsibility.

revise.why: To clarify and document the dependency on telemetry data to minimize coupling and ensure interface stability, and to establish interface versioning or abstraction layers for the Network Link to reduce impact of changes on the adaptive protocol logic.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol as designed sufficiently meets the customer-defined packet loss limit under operational conditions.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary with high cohesion and containment.
Verification through telemetry monitoring is feasible and reliable.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules causing minor coupling.
Changes in Network Link interfaces impacting the adaptive protocol logic.
evidence_needed: Documentation of telemetry data dependencies and interface contracts.
Verification plan including explicit steps to monitor and ensure packet loss criteria during teleoperation.
Interface versioning or abstraction layer implementation details.
